behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is model output quality from the speech-to-text/diarization model (LLM-DEP yes). A stubbed transcript with perfect labels would hide diarization failures (MOCK-HIDE yes). System concerns (HTTP call) exist, but correctness here is about who the model says spoke, so Agentic is the minimum. Adversarial critique: if this were only testing that the service passes through an already-labeled transcript unchanged, Integration would be enough; behavior text explicitly requires STT output preserving labels, which keeps it Agentic. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text targets one model stage (STT labeling), not a required multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real multi-speaker meeting audio sample and real STT API; assert transcript speaker labels are present and correctly distinguish attendees, and that those labels are available to downstream summarization input.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is local parsing/structuring logic with deterministic inputs/outputs (MOCK-MISS points to single-function logic). No real OS/network/subprocess dependency and no dependence on model judgment for this specific behavior. Adversarial critique: contract mismatches between parser module and caller could argue Integration, but behavior text focuses on extraction into structure, which can be validated at the parser boundary itself. Rejected higher level evidence: behavior text says 'Transcript parsing extracts...' and does not require live API/model calls.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parsing function/object directly with representative transcript text containing explicit assignments; assert extracted records include assignee and exact task text in the expected summary-input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model generation decisions about coverage/completeness of action items (LLM-DEP yes). Deterministic stubs would always satisfy mapping rules and miss omission failures (MOCK-HIDE yes). Adversarial critique: if this tested only a post-processing validator over already-produced action items, Integration would suffice; behavior text explicitly checks live summary output completeness, which is model-content dependent. Rejected higher level (Workflow): can be validated with one real summarization step from a fixed transcript without requiring multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript with known assignee-task pairs to the real summarization model and verify each assignee appears with at least one action item in the resulting structured summary.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Failure typically appears at module handoff: extractor output contract to schema assembler/router (MOCK-MISS yes at boundary). No required real OS/network/subprocess (Integration-brake a: no). No dependence on real model output quality because decisions are already identified in input fixture (b: no). No multi-agentic chain (c/d: no). Adversarial critique: if routing is a single pure function, Unit might be enough; behavior text references routing identified decisions into a section schema, implying cross-module wiring is the realistic failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Wire decision-identification output fixture through the real routing/assembly modules and assert identified decisions populate `decisions` fields and do not land in narrative text fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks semantic extraction/placement quality of live model output (LLM-DEP yes). Stubbing the model would hide real misses where decisions are omitted or misplaced (MOCK-HIDE yes). Adversarial critique: if only a deterministic formatter moved already-tagged decisions into sections, Integration would suffice; behavior text starts from transcript mentions and requires live summary placement, which depends on model judgment. Rejected higher level (Workflow): this can be validated in a single summarization pass from transcript input.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcript text containing clear decision statements; assert those decisions are present in `decisions` and absent from-only narrative placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires sequential multi-step processing across segments plus merged final output quality; this is multiple agentic decisions in sequence (MULTI-STEP yes). Mocking segment results hides real cross-segment coherence and carryover failures (MOCK-HIDE yes). Adversarial critique: if this only verified split counts and merge invocation order, Integration/System would suffice; behavior text includes 'single coherent summary,' which depends on real sequential model outputs and their merge.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute meeting recording, run full segmented processing with real STT/summarization, and assert segment order is respected and final merged summary preserves cross-segment action items/decisions coherently in one structured output.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly end-to-end with real recording and multiple model-dependent stages (transcribe then summarize), plus infrastructure handoffs; MULTI-STEP and LLM-DEP both yes. Any mocked stage can mask true pipeline failures. Adversarial critique: one might call this System because it uses real HTTP/subprocess boundaries, but behavior text requires full chained pipeline with AI-dependent outcomes, which exceeds pure System into Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like pipeline on a real meeting audio file: upload, real transcription, real summarization, and output delivery. Assert completion without human retries and that output conforms to required structured schema.
